name: Campfire Squid
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the squid by separating the head from the body and reserving the legs and ink sac. Chop the legs and reserve them.
2. Squeeze the ink from the sac over a small bowl and reserve it.
3. Remove quills from the body and peel off the outer skin of the squid. Set aside.
4. Heat half of the olive oil in a large skillet over medium coals or on the stovetop.
5. Add onion, garlic, lemon, bay leaves, thyme, paprika, salt, and pepper to the skillet. Cook until the onion is translucent.
6. Mix the ink with flour to make a paste. Add the chopped legs, half of the onion mixture from the pan, and half of the rice.
7. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Stuff the squid with the rice mixture and secure the opening with wood picks.
9. Heat the remaining onion mixture in the skillet.
10. Place the stuffed squid in the pan and cook, turning often, until golden on all sides, about 10 minutes.
11. Remove the squid and bay leaves from the pan. Spread any remaining rice in a layer in the pan.
12. Arrange the squid over the rice, cover, and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
13. Serve the squid over rice and garnish with parsley sprigs and lemon wedges, if desired.
